title: Conyza parva
intro: Conyza parva, also known as small fleabane or dwarf horseweed, is a small annual herb native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Conyza parva include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Conyza parva is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to reduce fever and inflammation.

uses: Conyza parva is used in traditional medicine, particularly in various Asian cultures, for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and other respiratory issues. In addition, the plant is used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and nausea. Its leaves and stems are often used in herbal remedies, either fresh or dried, to make teas or infusions. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Conyza parva for medicinal purposes.
